release_card.summary: 0.0.13-pre promotes optional SSL manifests into a bounded Skill Registry evidence layer, adds Way clarification interviews, improves App/CLI metadata-source visibility, and packages managed-agent SSL manifests with release assets.
documented_flow: repo/document graph and memory target selection
Codex app/CLI boxed LongWay status fallback
compact opened/running/completed/closed subagent lifecycle transcript events
validation: Skill Registry and SSL manifest tests cover managed-agent manifest coverage, missing/invalid fallback, stale version, and skill_id drift
routing tests cover registry-backed Scheduling evidence and display metadata source labels
review-policy tests cover registry-backed Logical risk precheck and Arbiter ownership for high-risk work
Way tests cover Structural planning context, persisted clarification requests, and one-time answer consumption
app-panel tests cover planned-row source labels and compact target-root retry commands
release script checks passed for bash syntax, builder asset-name smoke, and temporary package extraction with all 8 SSL manifests
release-target cargo builds passed for darwin arm64, darwin x86_64, linux arm64, linux x86_64, and windows x86_64
scripts/release/verify-release-asset-matrix.sh passed for 5 assets and packaged SSL manifest coverage
PowerShell was unavailable on the maintainer macOS host, so Windows install smoke used static install.ps1 surface checks
Linux and Windows assets are package-verifiable on macOS but require native runtime validation on their target OSes
deferred: native Codex App right progress panel mounting remains host-owned
Linux and Windows native runtime validation remains target-OS dependent
